graded_findings.rationale: The gather marked this routing update as appropriate for the new log sampler work. It does not describe an adherence gap.
The gather found this module overview was updated to include LogSampler ownership and routing. That is not a merge-blocking documentation problem.
The gather found this new design doc covers sample rates, derivation, propagation, and hot-path constraints. No substantive docs gap is identified for this ref.

The gather reports the obsolete client-side DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT entry was removed consistently with worker-authoritative sampling. This is an appropriate update, not a finding that should block merge.
The gather reports this user documentation now describes the new random sampling rates and configuration entrypoints. No remaining issue is asserted for this file.
The gather found the dscli docs were updated from log_rate_limit to the new sample-rate options and derivation semantics. This item is not an adherence gap.

This shared logging design doc still describes the removed log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, and LogRateLimiter register-sync model. Leaving core repo context stale for the new LogSampler model is a real docs coherence gap.

This context doc still assigns sampling decisions to Trace and LogRateLimiter semantics that the gather says have been superseded. That can mislead future infrastructure work around ownership and propagation.

This plan still frames slow PLOG bypass behavior around log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter instead of the new diagnostic sampling path. The mismatch is substantive because it affects operational logging behavior documentation.
